headline: She Started With A $5 Lamp And A Stroller — Now Runs $5.2M Platform And Owns Two $300K Rental Homes
summary: A $5 church-yard sale lamp turned a young mom's weekend thrifting trip into a full-time business. Jocelyn Elizabeth, creator of YouTube channel "Crazy Lamp Lady" and founder of resale marketplace NikNax, told CNBC she began flipping thrifted finds in 2011...

headline: 2 Internet Stocks to Research Further and 1 We Ignore
summary: By breaking down physical barriers, consumer internet businesses are reshaping how people shop, connect, learn, and play. This influence cuts both ways though because they have high exposure to the ups and downs of consumer spending, and the market seems to believe the tide is turning in the wrong direction - over the past six months, the industry has tumbled by 16.6%. This drop is a stark contrast from the S&P 500’s 7.9% gain.

headline: How the BFC plans to future-proof British fashion by 2030
summary: British Fashion Council CEO, Laura Weir, tells CNBC’s Tania Bryer about the launch of its new strategy - BFC 2030: Access, Creativity, Growth. She explains how the organization is shifting from “promoting” British fashion to “supporting” it, as the UK luxury and retail sector navigates global economic headwinds, post-Brexit pressures and changing consumer habits.
